content: Start the CBO intervention profile for Porto Alegre. Use the /cbo-intervention skill flow. Always use the ask_user tool for multiple-choice questions. In your first message, mention that the user can drop existing documents (proposals, reports, plans, photos) into the chat at any time — you'll extract info and auto-fill sections.

Hello! 👋 I'm here to help you document your community organization's nature-based solutions project in Porto Alegre.

**Before we start:** You can drop any existing documents into our chat at any time — project proposals, reports, plans, budget spreadsheets, site photos, or anything else you have. I'll read them and automatically fill in the profile sections to save you time!

Let me start by learning about your organization. We'll go through 6 phases together:

1. **Who We Are** — Your organization and team
2. **Where We Work** — Your intervention site and community
3. **What We're Doing** — Your climate solution and activities  
4. **What We Need** — Resources, support, and partnerships
5. **Results & Evidence** — What you've achieved so far
6. **Review & Score** — Investment readiness assessment

Let's begin! 🌱
Community-Based Organization; Associacao Vila Flores

Great! Let me start documenting the intervention profile for **Associação Vila Flores**, a community-based organization in Porto Alegre.

I'll begin by setting us to Phase 1 and gathering some initial information about your organization.

📄 **Tip:** You can drop any existing documents (project proposals, reports, presentations) into this chat at any time, and I'll automatically extract and fill in relevant information!

---

## Phase 1: Who We Are 👥

I've recorded **Associação Vila Flores** as a community-based organization. Now I'd like to learn more about your team, mission, and experience. Please answer the questions above!
5-10 years; Community development; 16-50 people; Mix of paid and volunteers

Excellent! Associação Vila Flores is clearly an established organization with meaningful experience and a solid team. Let me update your profile and score some maturity metrics based on what you've shared.

Perfect! Your organization shows strong capacity — 5-10 years of community development work with a sizable mixed team is a great foundation. I've scored your **Organizational Delivery Capacity** as 2/3 (proven project management).

Now I'd like to learn more about your experience with environmental projects and managing external funding — this will help us understand your readiness for NBS implementation.
Yes, multiple projects; Yes, once or twice

Ótimo! A Associação Vila Flores tem um histórico sólido de entrega de projetos e experiência com financiamento. Isso mostra capacidade organizacional forte! 🌱

Let me record this and continue gathering information about your organization.

Perfect! I've recorded your organization's project and funding experience and scored your delivery capacity (2/3 - showing strong demonstrated project management). 

Now I have two quick questions:

1. **Contact information** - Who should be the main contact person for this project?
2. **Technical experience** - Does your team have any members with technical experience in nature-based solutions like tree planting, wetland restoration, or rain gardens?
Prefiro adicionar depois; Sim, temos especialistas técnicos
Ótimo! Isso mostra que a Associação Vila Flores já tem uma base sólida de experiência e conta com conhecimento técnico na equipe — isso é um diferencial importante!

Great! Now let's move to **Phase 2: Where We Work** — this is where we identify the specific location and community context for your intervention.

I'm going to open an **interactive map** so you can show me exactly where the Associação Vila Flores operates and which sites you're targeting for your NBS project. 

On the map, you'll be able to:
1. **First**, select the neighborhood/zone where you work
2. **Then**, mark the specific parks, schools, green spaces, or wetlands you're focusing on

The map will also show you climate risk layers (flood risk, heatwave risk) so you can see how your site relates to Porto Alegre's environmental challenges.

🗺️ **The map is now open!** 

Please:
1. **Click on your neighborhood** (Floresta / 4º Distrito area)
2. **Then mark the specific sites** where you're implementing or planning NBS projects

Take your time — you can toggle the flood risk and heatwave layers to see how climate hazards overlap with your work area. When you're done selecting, let me know!

Map selection (composite mode):
- [zone] Jardim Floresta:  risk, intervention: , area: ? km², population: ?, flood: 0%, heat: 0%, at (-30.0022, -51.1590)
- [osm] Parque 20 de Maio (drawn area) at (-30.0171, -51.1434)
- [osm] Parque Alim Pedro (drawn area) at (-30.0142, -51.1750)
Total: 3 assets, 0 sampled points

Excelente! Obrigado pela seleção no mapa. Vejo que vocês trabalham no bairro **Jardim Floresta** e selecionaram dois parques importantes:
- **Parque 20 de Maio**
- **Parque Alim Pedro**

Vou registrar essas informações e depois fazer algumas perguntas sobre os locais onde vocês atuam.

Perfect! I've recorded that you're working in the **Jardim Floresta neighborhood** with two parks:
- **Parque 20 de Maio**
- **Parque Alim Pedro**

Now I'd like to understand the current conditions at these sites. Please answer the question above about what these locations are like today! 🌳
Área verde existente, mas degradada, Parque bem mantido, mas com risco de enchente
Perfeito! Vocês trabalham com dois tipos de parque diferentes. Vou registrar isso e agora vamos entender mais sobre as pessoas que vivem perto desses locais e como vocês se conectam com a comunidade.

Ótimo! Essas informações vão me ajudar a entender:
- **Quem se beneficia** dos parques que vocês estão trabalhando
- **Como a comunidade participa** — isso é muito importante para avaliar o nível de "ancoragem comunitária" do projeto

Aguardo suas respostas! 🌳
